Recipes

For your harvest

Slow-roasted tomatoes with thyme & garlic
90 min · serves 4
from your harvest
Ingredients
  • 1 kg ripe tomatoes, halved
  • 4 garlic cloves, sliced
  • Olive oil
  • Fresh thyme
  • Sea salt & black pepper
Method

Arrange tomatoes cut-side up on a tray. Scatter garlic and thyme, drizzle generously with olive oil, season. Roast at 150°C for about 90 minutes, until soft and lightly caramelized at the edges.

Classic tomato sauce
45 min · makes 3 jars
from your harvest
Ingredients
  • 1.5 kg tomatoes, chopped
  • 1 onion, diced
  • 3 garlic cloves
  • Olive oil
  • Fresh basil, salt
Method

Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil until soft. Add tomatoes and simmer uncovered for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally. Blend for a smooth sauce, or leave chunky. Finish with basil and salt.

Summer tomato & mozzarella salad
15 min · serves 2
from your harvest
Ingredients
  • 500 g tomatoes, sliced
  • Fresh mozzarella
  • Fresh basil leaves
  • Olive oil & balsamic vinegar
  • Flaky salt
Method

Layer sliced tomatoes and mozzarella on a plate with basil leaves. Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic vinegar, finish with flaky salt.

Plant check

Check a plant

Take a photo

Analyzing …
Tomato — underwatered
Drooping, yellowing lower leaves
87% confidence
How to fix it
  • —Water deeply at the base, early morning
  • —Add mulch around the stem to hold moisture
  • —Check drainage holes aren't blocked
  • —Avoid misting leaves in direct sun

Tips this month
Ventilate on hot days

Check the watering routine

Community

Grower feed

SR
Sara
Evening in the greenhouse
Sara spend most evenings out here now — the light through the glass in the evening is what sold me on it in the first place. Tea, dahlias from the cutting patch behind it, and no rush to go back inside 🌿
ML
Malte
Lights up for the season
Malte put the lights up along the roof edge last weekend — it's turned into the one corner of the garden everyone gathers in once the sun goes down, greenhouse and all. First frost can't be far off now, but this thing keeps the tomatoes going weeks longer than the open bed ever did.
PT
Peter
Tucked into the garden
Peter two years in and it's completely disappeared into the garden now — lilac on one side, the old apple tree leaning over the roof. Still my favorite spot on the whole property, rain or shine.
